title: Karpatiosorbus slovenica
intro: Karpatiosorbus slovenica, also known as Slovenian Mountain Whitebeam, is a rare and endangered deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Alps. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis. Karpatiosorbus slovenica contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, as well as other conditions such as digestive problems and fever.
taxonomy: Karpatiosorbus slovenica is a species in the genus Karpatiosorbus, which is a group of flowering plants in the family Rosaceae. This genus is closely related to Sorbus and has similarities in its fruit and leaf morphology. Karpatiosorbus slovenica is native to the Balkan region, particularly in Slovenia. The classification of Karpatiosorbus slovenica is further supported by its unique characteristics such as its white or pink flowers and red or yellowish fruits. As a member of the Rosaceae family, Karpatiosorbus slovenica is related to other plants like roses, apples, and pears.
